text: In the meeting, you opened the floor and handled objections.
In a presentation, you guide the audience from start to finish.

Okay, Eduardo, let's rehearse your opening. Give me the overview first, as if I were the audience.
Sure. I would like to begin by giving you a quick overview. First, I will cover the market. Then I will walk you through the plan.
Good outline. But you jumped from the market to the plan with no signpost. The transition was not clear.
You're right. Let me add one: Moving on to our results. And then, before I move on, I will highlight the key number.
Much better. And how will you close? The audience always remembers the last thing they hear.
To sum up, I will leave them with one clear takeaway: the market is growing, and we are ready.
Let me start by giving you a quick overview.
First, I will cover the market, and then our results.
Moving on to the plan for the year ahead.
I would like to highlight one key number on this slide.
To sum up, the key takeaway is that our market is growing.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for being here. I'm Eduardo Chiba, Head of the B2B Unit at Quinto Andar. Let me start by giving you a quick overview of today's presentation. First, I'll cover the market and where the opportunity is. After that, I'll move on to our results from the last quarter. Then I'd like to walk you through our plan for the year ahead. Finally, I'll share the three key takeaways I want you to remember. The presentation will take about twenty minutes, and there will be time for questions at the end. So, to begin, let's look at the market. The B2B real estate segment is growing fast, and the audience for our product is larger than ever. Keep that picture in mind, because everything I show you today connects back to it.

Okay, this is just a quick rehearsal of my opening. Let me try it again. Good afternoon, everyone, and thanks for joining. I'd like to begin by giving you a short overview of where we are. First, I'll set the scene with the market. After that, I'll move on to the numbers. Then I'll walk you through the plan, and finally I'll leave you with three clear takeaways. I think the structure is good, but I am speaking too fast. I need to slow down and pause after each signpost, so the audience can follow the transition. The opening sets the tone for the whole presentation, so it has to be calm and confident. One more time, slower this time, and then I'll be ready.

First, you open and give a short overview of the whole presentation. Then you set the scene and explain why the topic matters to the audience. After that, you walk the audience through each main point in order. Next, you signpost every transition, so people always know where they are. Finally, you sum up the key takeaways and invite questions.
